rows.translation: 提克里斯有没有泰西斯能想到的敌人呢？
rows.raw: "Does Tychris have any enemies Tethys can think of."

rows.translation: 城市现状

rows.original: How are things in the city?
rows.translation: 城里的情况怎么样？
返回

rows.original: "About me? Surely I am not a suspect!" You shake your head. "I'm just looking for [pc.isCunning |context |a clearer picture ] here." A momentary bristle passes from the Lady Consort's features, and her shoulders relax backwards. "Very well. Of course. I will spare you the obvious pageantry, then." She takes a breath, shaking off the severity of her normal courtly demeanor. "My family are landholders north of the city, on the frontier of Tychris's domain. We are its shield against the barbarians of the tundra. But we were not immune to the decline that has affected so many bloodlines in the principality; Kythie is the last of my line, and I doubt very much that I would be alive if it were not for my father's love for a foreign priestess." "The decline?" Tethys sighs. "An affliction has long gripped Tychris. One of the malingering repercussions of the Godswar, still blighting us centuries after. The bloodlines of our nobility run thinner each year, producing weaker offspring, and fewer of them. I love my husband very much, [pc.name], but go outside and look upon the statue of his ancestor-king who won the holy war alongside Nareva herself; then compare him to Evander. Prince Philandros would have thought Evander to be his smallest daughter at a glance." [pc.bg scholar |You've heard of such things happening in other isolated reaches of the old Empire. Some consider it to be a punishment from the gods for inbreeding. ] She laughs, but there's a fond smile on her face. Tethys crosses her arms beneath her prodigious bosom — there's certainly no thinning of her bloodline to worry about, if that manifests as skinny and frail heirs. "So your father found the cure?" "We believe that foreign blood will reinvigorate the city. That is why Father took a Jassiran to be his partner, and why we have reached out to Felurze to wed Kythie. Other noble families are beginning to follow suit, in the wake of Kythie's public engagement. I am glad to have been a part of this, in my small part. Perhaps even moreso than my pride as a soldier. " "Did you follow your father's footsteps, as a shield of the realm?" That gives a proud chest-puff out of the 'mander. "I did! I am Strategos for my husband, master of his soldiers. I trained for the position since I was a child, I fought against many foes before I married Evander, and it is my honor to have protected Tychris during the Lion Witch's War afterwards." "Tell me about that?" "Ah, I should not be surprised the war's not so famous beyond our borders," Tethys says, trying to mask some amount of disappointment. "About eighteen years ago, a group of leothran barbarians descended from the northern tundra and began raiding the frontier villages. They enslaved our citizens, burned farms, destroyed bridges that connect us to our people. A shamaness lead the pack, much more powerful than any that had threatened the city for generations — strong enough to lay waste to village militias all by herself. The Order Astrida recalled its paladins to hunt her, but before they could march, a tribesman defected to us and told us of his kins' movements, of their plans to celebrate the solstice in an isolated valley near my family's holdings; I was in a position to seize the initiative and thrust north with the princely guard under cover of night while the Astrida were still coming to their camps." There's a certain relish in Tethys's voice now. "A hard march, but I know the land well. We moved with speed, and caught the witch and her allies while they were still hung over from their merry-making, barely ready for battle — the state in which they had once found our people. Even still, the witch was powerful! She shattered the forest around us with thunder and scoured our shields with lightning. Still and all, we fell on her like warhawks!" The salamander's fists clench before her, and her tail blazes bright. "My men fought with unequalled valor, driving the tribe's warriors to route, until I found myself locking blades with the Lion Witch herself. She gave me the greatest battle of my life! Staff and spear clashed, and her spells burnt my shield black, burned it to cinders. I thought I had finally met my match, until in her hubris she left her spellcasting hand exposed, ready for a killing cantrip; I skewered it with my war knife!" Tethys allows herself a momentary, sadistic smirk. "How she howled! A little pain crippled a mage that powerful, forced her to flee." "Did you kill her?" "No," Tethys sighs. "She did not die by my hand. Her kinsmen broke, bearing their wounded shaman back to the tundra... and her thane-husband left me a terrible wound in trying to separate us. Still, the leothrans were scattered by my guard, and the witch proved easy prey for the Astrida to hunt, once the warband was routed." [pc.bg soldier hunter barbarian |A fine story... though you know tribal cultures like that tend to hold grudges for generations. A humiliating defeat might leave them bitter even today. |Well, routing a whole barbarian tribe is certainly an accomplishment. ] "Did those leothrans ever give you trouble again?" "No, thank the goddess. We taught them a painful lesson, and it's bought us a generation of peace from our neighbors in the north. And that remains my second greatest accomplishment." "And what's the first?" Tethys looks at the door and smiles. "You can guess, I'm sure."
rows.translation: 关于我？我肯定不是嫌疑人吧！” 你摇了摇头，说道：“我只是想了解一下而已。 [pc.isCunning |背景信息 |更清晰的画面 ] 好吧。” 王妃的脸色瞬间变得柔和起来，她的肩膀也放松了下来。“当然可以。那我就不搞那些繁琐的仪式了。”她深吸一口气，摆脱了平时那种正式、端庄的举止，显得更加自然了。 我的家族拥有城北的土地，位于提克里斯领地的边境。我们是抵御苔原上那些野蛮人的屏障。然而，我们也无法逃脱这场影响整个公国众多家族的衰败趋势；凯西是我家族的最后一代成员，如果没有我父亲对那位外国女祭司的深情，我恐怕早已不在人世了。这种衰败……” 泰西斯叹了口气：“提克里斯长期以来一直深受其害。这是神战留下的后遗症之一，几个世纪以来一直在折磨着我们。我们贵族的血脉一年比一年稀薄，后代也越来越弱小，数量也越来越少。 我非常爱我的丈夫。 [pc.name]但是，你不妨走到外面去看看他那位曾与娜蕾瓦一同赢得那场神圣战争的祖先国王的雕像，然后再将他与埃万德进行比较。菲兰德罗斯王子一看就会觉得，埃万德简直就像他的小女儿一样。 [pc.bg scholar |你肯定听说过，在旧帝国的其他偏远地区，也发生过类似的事情。 有些人认为，这是上天对近亲繁殖行为的惩罚。 ] 她笑了，但脸上带着一丝温暖的笑容。泰西斯将双臂交叉在自己那丰满的胸前——如果这意味着她的后代会变得瘦弱不堪，那么她的血统显然并没有退化。“所以你父亲找到了治愈方法？我们相信，外来的血液能够为这座城市注入新的活力。正因为如此，父亲才会选择一位贾西兰人作为自己的伴侣，也正因为如此，我们才会联系费卢尔泽家族，让凯西与他们的女儿结婚。在凯西公开宣布订婚之后，其他贵族家族也开始纷纷效仿这一做法。 能够以自己微小的力量参与到这件事中来，我感到非常荣幸。或许，这种自豪感甚至超过了作为一名军人所感受到的自豪。 你是追随你父亲的脚步，成为这个王国的守护者吗？” 听到这个问题，她自豪地挺起了胸膛：“是的！我是我丈夫的军师，是他军队的统帅。从小我就为这个职位而努力训练，在嫁给埃万德之前，我已经与许多敌人交过战。而在后来的‘狮女巫之战’中，能够保护提克里斯，对我来说是一种荣誉。 跟我说说这件事吧？” “啊，战争在我们国境之外并不出名，这也难怪，”泰西斯说道，试图掩饰自己内心的些许失望，“大约十八年前，一群来自北方苔原的莱奥斯兰野蛮人开始袭击边境的村庄。 他们奴役了我们的公民，烧毁了农田，毁坏了连接我们与同胞们的桥梁。有一位女萨满率领着这些侵略者，她的力量远超那些几代以来一直威胁这座城市的敌人——她强大到足以独自消灭所有村庄的民兵组织。 阿斯特里达教团召回了它的骑士们去追捕她，但在他们出发之前，有位部落成员投奔了我们，他向我们透露了他族人的动向——他们打算在我家族领地附近的某个偏远山谷里庆祝至日节。因此，我有机会先发制人，在夜幕的掩护下率领王室卫队向北方进发，而那时阿斯特里达的骑士们还正在返回他们的营地呢。" 泰西斯的语气中现在带着一丝兴奋。“虽然路程艰辛，但我对这片土地非常熟悉。我们迅速行进，在女巫和她的盟友们还沉浸在欢乐之中、几乎还没准备好战斗的时候就抓住了他们——他们当初就是以这样的状态袭击了我们的人。即便如此，那女巫依然实力强大！她用雷电摧毁了我们周围的森林，用闪电击碎了我们的盾牌。但尽管如此，我们还是像战鹰一样扑向了她！ 蝾螈将双拳紧握在身前，它的尾巴也亮起了炽烈的光芒。“我的部下们以无与伦比的勇气作战，迫使敌方的战士们纷纷退避，直到我最终与那位狮女巫展开了决斗。那场战斗，成为了我一生中最难忘的经历！ 她的法杖与我的长矛相撞，她的法术将我的护盾烧得通黑，直至化为灰烬。我原以为自己终于遇到了对手，然而就在她傲慢自大的那一刻，她露出了释放法术的手掌，正好让我使出致命的诡计——我用战刀刺中了她的手！泰西斯暗自露出了一丝幸灾乐祸的笑容。‘她当时叫得多惨啊！仅仅是一点痛苦，就让那样强大的法师陷入了困境，迫使她不得不逃跑。 是你杀了她吗？” “不，”泰西斯叹息道，“她并不是死在我的手里。她的族人溃败了，他们把受伤的萨满带回了苔原……而她的丈夫在试图将我们分开时，给了我一个致命的伤。不过，我的卫兵已经把那些莱奥斯人驱散了，而当那支战帮被击溃后，那个女巫也就成了阿斯特里达轻易捕捉到的猎物。 [pc.bg soldier hunter barbarian |一个不错的故事……不过 你知道，这样的部落文化往往会让怨恨延续数代人。一次耻辱性的失败可能会让他们至今仍心怀怨恨。 |嗯，将整个野蛮人部落都纳入自己的统治范围，这确实是一项了不起的成就。 ] 那些利奥特兰人后来还给你惹过麻烦吗？” “没有，多亏了女神。我们给了他们一个惨痛的教训，因此我们北边的邻居们在这一代人里都没有再给我们带来麻烦。而这一点，可以说是我第二大的成就。” “那第一大的成就是什么呢？” 泰西斯望了望门口，笑了笑：“你应该能猜到的，我相信。

" "Did you follow your father's footsteps, as a shield of the realm?" That gives a proud chest-puff out of the 'mander. "I did! I am Strategos for my husband, master of his soldiers. I trained for the position since I was a child, I fought against many foes before I married Evander, and it is my honor to have protected Tychris during the Lion Witch's War afterwards." "Tell me about that?" "Ah, I should not be surprised the war's not so famous beyond our borders," Tethys says, trying to mask some amount of disappointment. "About eighteen years ago, a group of leothran barbarians descended from the northern tundra and began raiding the frontier villages. They enslaved our citizens, burned farms, destroyed bridges that connect us to our people. A shamaness lead the pack, much more powerful than any that had threatened the city for generations — strong enough to lay waste to village militias all by herself. The Order Astrida recalled its paladins to hunt her, but before they could march, a tribesman defected to us and told us of his kins' movements, of their plans to celebrate the solstice in an isolated valley near my family's holdings; I was in a position to seize the initiative and thrust north with the princely guard under cover of night while the Astrida were still coming to their camps." There's a certain relish in Tethys's voice now. "A hard march, but I know the land well. We moved with speed, and caught the witch and her allies while they were still hung over from their merry-making, barely ready for battle — the state in which they had once found our people. Even still, the witch was powerful! She shattered the forest around us with thunder and scoured our shields with lightning. Still and all, we fell on her like warhawks!" The salamander's fists clench before her, and her tail blazes bright. "My men fought with unequalled valor, driving the tribe's warriors to route, until I found myself locking blades with the Lion Witch herself. She gave me the greatest battle of my life! Staff and spear clashed, and her spells burnt my shield black, burned it to cinders. I thought I had finally met my match, until in her hubris she left her spellcasting hand exposed, ready for a killing cantrip; I skewered it with my war knife!" Tethys allows herself a momentary, sadistic smirk. "How she howled! A little pain crippled a mage that powerful, forced her to flee." "Did you kill her?" "No," Tethys sighs. "She did not die by my hand. Her kinsmen broke, bearing their wounded shaman back to the tundra... and her thane-husband left me a terrible wound in trying to separate us. Still, the leothrans were scattered by my guard, and the witch proved easy prey for the Astrida to hunt, once the warband was routed." [pc.bg soldier hunter barbarian |A fine story... though you know tribal cultures like that tend to hold grudges for generations. A humiliating defeat might leave them bitter even today. |Well, routing a whole barbarian tribe is certainly an accomplishment. ] "Did those leothrans ever give you trouble again?" "No, thank the goddess. We taught them a painful lesson, and it's bought us a generation of peace from our neighbors in the north. And that remains my second greatest accomplishment." "And what's the first?" Tethys looks at the door and smiles. "You can guess, I'm sure." '

rows.translation: 关于她自己

rows.original: "Hm, I am not inclined to gossip about my family, Champion," Tethys says. "But Evander's ancestors have ruled this city for four hundred years, since its initial founding. From Belharan suzerainty, through the Godswar, and now more than two centuries of independence. I should say that have done well by this city, and they are well loved by the people within it." "Tactful as ever," you say, "but I was hoping for something more personal. Tell me about your husband, at least?" You can see a moment of hesitation cross Tethys's face, before her features finally soften. "My dear flame. We have been companions since childhood, him and I. Our fathers were close as brothers, and though I was a woman grown before they finally decided we should be wed, I knew all my life I was to be his shield. His Strategos." The statement makes Tethys swell with pride, all the way to her tail-flame. "Were you in love?" Tethys's smile only deepens. "I couldn't tell you when it began, but yes, I think so. For a long time. There is a deep comfort in being with someone for so long, knowing someone so well. Truly sharing your life with them." Tethys pauses, resting a hand over her heart. "We were both delighted when our parents made the decision." "Were there any other contenders for Evander's hand? Or yours?" "A few," Tethys says. "For Evander's, anyway. I had no serious suitors of my own. But my husband is still a prince, and every prince will have those who wish to bind themselves to his power. Only one was seriously considered from here in Tychris, if your reasoning is what I suspect: Lady Zainem, a sorceress of the court, from a long lineage of magical excellence. She is a good friend to our family, though also one of our harshest critics, whenever our edicts and her philosophy clash. I would hardly suspect her of treachery!" [pc.bg scholar |Lady Zainem... you vaguely recognize the name from your readings back home. She's written a few treatise on the relationship between the health of the body and the mind; that one cannot be a powerful sorcerer while possessing a frail physical form. Very focused on exercise and meditation. ] "So every other suitor was from other cities?" Tethys nods. "Yes, and most have their own husbands now. It's been two decades, [pc.name]. I doubt any of them would feel slighted over a man they hardly knew that long." Fair enough. "Alright. Aside from the three of you, is there anyone else in the royal family?" "We are... a small family, [pc.name]. My injuries from the Lion Witch's War precluded me from having more children than Kythie, and Evander had only one sibling of his own, now passed from illness. We have two nieces, twins, just a year older than Kythie. Marianthe and Nephele. They and their mother chiefly reside in an estate outside the city that the family owns." "Do they get along well with Kythie?" Tethys smiles. "Oh, yes. They all love each other dearly." [pc.bg noblescion minstrel |Be that as it may, those girls would be the heirs to the city if something happened to Kythie. They certainly have something to gain from her insanity... |Then you suppose they're not the most likely suspects. ]
rows.translation: 嗯，冠军，我并不喜欢谈论我的家族，”泰西斯说道，“但埃万德的祖先从这座城市建立之初就开始统治它，至今已经四百年了。从属于贝尔哈兰的统治，经过神战时期，到现在已经有两个多世纪的独立统治。我必须承认，他们的统治对这座城市很有好处，也深受当地人民的爱戴。 你还是那么圆滑得体，”你说，“但我本来希望能听到一些更私人的一些事情。至少，跟我说说你的丈夫吧？” 你可以看到泰西斯脸上闪过一丝犹豫，但很快她的表情又变得柔和起来。“我亲爱的…… 我们从小就是一起长大的伙伴，他和我。我们的父亲情同手足，虽然在我长大成人之后他们才最终决定让我们结婚，但我从一开始就明白，自己一生的使命就是成为他的保护者，他的得力助手。这句话让泰西斯感到无比自豪，这种自豪感甚至蔓延到了她的尾焰上。 你曾经爱过吗？” 泰西斯的笑容更加深了。“我无法确切地说这种感情是从什么时候开始的，但没错，我想我确实爱过。而且持续了很长时间。与某个人共度如此漫长的时光，如此深入地了解对方，真正与他们分享自己的生活，这种感觉真的很令人欣慰。”泰西斯稍作停顿，将手放在心上，“当我们的父母做出这个决定时，我们俩都感到非常高兴。 还有其他人争夺埃万德的手吗？或者争夺你的手的人？” “有几个，”泰西斯回答，“至少是争夺埃万德的手的人。至于我，没有认真的追求者。不过我的丈夫毕竟还是位王子，每个王子身边都会有一些人想要依附于他的权势。 如果我的推测没错的话，在提克里斯，真正被认真考虑过的人只有一个人：那就是宫廷女巫扎伊妮姆女士。她出身于一个拥有悠久魔法传承的家族。她是我们家的好朋友，但每当我们的法令与她的理念发生冲突时，她也会成为我们最严厉的批评者之一。 我绝对不会怀疑她会背叛我！ [pc.bg scholar |扎伊奈姆女士……这个名字在你在家乡读过的书籍中曾出现过。她写过几篇关于身体健康与精神状态之间关系的论文，认为如果身体状况不佳，就无法成为一名强大的巫师。她非常重视锻炼和冥想。 ] 所以其他追求她的人都是来自其他城市的吗？” 泰西斯点了点头。“是的，而且他们中的大多数现在都已经有了自己的丈夫。毕竟已经过去二十年了…… [pc.name]我怀疑他们中的任何人，都不会因为一个他们才认识不久的男人而感到被冒犯。” 有道理。“好吧。除了你们三个人之外，王室里还有其他人吗？” “我们……是一个小家庭。 [pc.name]在‘狮女之战’中受的伤让我无法再生育其他孩子，而埃文德也只有一个兄弟姐妹，不过他已经因病去世了。我们有两个侄女，是一对双胞胎，她们比凯西大一岁，名叫玛丽安特和内菲尔。她们和她们的母亲主要住在家里在城外拥有的一处庄园里。” “她们和凯西相处得很好吗？” 泰西斯微笑着回答：“哦，当然。她们彼此都非常喜欢。 [pc.bg noblescion minstrel |尽管如此， 如果凯西出了什么事，那些女孩就会成为这座城市的继承人。他们肯定能从她的疯狂行为中得到一些好处…… |那么你认为他们应该不是最有可能的嫌疑人吧。 ]

They certainly have something to gain from her insanity... |Then you suppose they're not the most likely suspects. ] '

rows.translation: 关于王室家族

rows.original: The question makes Tethys visibly uncomfortable. Her tail dims, and she looks to the floor. "I should have noticed it sooner, [pc.name]. But Kythie's at that age where, well, the heart begins to yearn in ways it hadn't before..." Tethys sighs heavily, voice dropping quiet. "We just thought she was learning things about herself, you must understand. We were upset, but-" "Slow down, slow down. What happened?" Tethys scratches at her arm scales. "I planted the aurum wreathe on my daughter's head at the Tycathalon this year, [pc.name], won through her hard work, not her bloodline. A high honor for us both. Something she could boast about the rest of her life. What she should not have done is grab hold of the runner-up and kiss on her on the stage in front of the entire city." That could certainly cause a kerfuffle, yes. "The worst part is, the city cheered for it! They loved seeing their future ruler letting loose. If only they knew!" Tethys groans, rubbing her temples. "The woman in question gave Kythie a piece of her mind, as did her parents, behind the scenes. That seemed to be that. A few days later, a blizzard rolled through and trapped us all indoors... and then we had one of our most trusted maidservants begging a private audience to report Kythie attempting to seduce her." "Twice so quickly... did you think something was amiss?" "Not amiss, per se," Tethys says awkwardly. "We punished her for her behavior, of course. Unwanted advances are beneath a princess of the realm. But in truth, Evander and I were preparing for a more serious talk about her future. We didn't attribute her behavior to external factors yet." You quirk a brow. "Talk about what?" Tethys shifts her weight and glances at the door. "That perhaps she would struggle in her marriage commitments to the prince of Felurze, if it was two women she was pressing herself upon in these moments of vulnerability." "Ah." "But, of course, our fears were misplaced. We restricted Kythie to her chambers, only for her to fall upon one of the guards soon after. She's strong, [pc.name]. Strong enough to hurt a grown man trained for combat. But she is also a gentle soul. That is when we knew something was truly amiss." No kidding. "And she's been in this tower ever since, huh?" "With one reprieve. When the Order Astrida first unbound the curse..."
rows.translation: 这个问题让泰西斯明显感到不安。她的尾巴颜色暗了下来，她把目光投向了地面。“我本应该早点注意到的…… [pc.name]但是凯西现在正处于那个阶段，嗯，她的心开始以一种前所未有的方式渴望着某些东西……”泰西斯沉重地叹了口气，声音渐渐低了下去，“我们只是以为她在了解关于自己的一些事情，你一定要理解。我们当时确实很担心，但是——” “慢点，慢慢说。到底发生了什么？” 泰西斯挠了挠自己手臂上的鳞片，“今年在提卡塔隆节上，我在女儿的头上戴上了金色的花环…… [pc.name]这一荣誉是她通过自己的努力获得的，而不是凭借她的出身。这对我们双方来说都是一件非常光荣的事情。这也是她可以终生引以为傲的成就。她确实应该如此。 不是 他们所做的就是抓住那位亚军，在整个城市的注视下，在舞台上亲了她一下。” 这确实可能会引起一阵骚动，没错。 “最糟糕的是，全城的市民都为这一幕欢呼！他们很高兴看到自己未来的统治者如此放纵自己。 要是他们知道该多好啊！”泰西斯呻吟着，揉着太阳穴。“那个女人当面严厉地批评了凯西，她的父母也在背后责备了她。事情似乎就这样结束了。然而几天后，一场暴风雪来袭，把我们都困在了室内。然后，我们有一位最值得信赖的女仆请求单独会面，报告说Kythie试图引诱她。” “这么快就发生了两次……你是不是觉得有些不对劲？” “严格来说，并没有什么不对劲，”Tethys有些尴尬地回答道。 我们当然因为她的行为而惩罚了她。这种不必要的冒犯行为，根本不配一个王国的公主做出。但实际上，埃文德和我原本打算就她的未来进行一次更严肃的谈话。我们当时还没有将她的行为归因于外部因素。你挑了挑眉毛：“说什么？” 泰西斯挪了挪身子，瞥了一眼门：“也许她在履行婚姻责任时会遇到困难…… 王子 关于费卢尔泽的事情……在那些脆弱的时刻，她其实是靠两个人来支撑自己的。” “啊。” “不过，当然了，我们的担忧完全是多余的。我们把凯西关在她的房间里，结果她不久之后还是遇到了其中一名守卫。她其实很坚强…… [pc.name]这种力量足以伤害一个受过战斗训练的成年男子。但她也拥有一颗温柔的心。 那个 就在那时，我们意识到真的出了什么问题。” “说真的……从那以后，她就一直待在这座塔里，对吧？” “只有一次例外。当阿斯特里达教团第一次解除了那个诅咒的时候……

rows.translation: 关于这个诅咒

rows.original: "Tychris bears the honor of hosting Nareva's grandest temple north of the Holy City itself," Tethys says bitterly, "but they have only offered false hope, when they offered any at all! I-" Tethys cuts herself short, balling her fists. For a moment you worry that she'll be the one striking the door, but a heartbeat passes, and she merely rests her palm against the wood. "I apologize," Tethys finally says. "But I have grown more than frustrated with our divine patrons. Each and every day since the curse began, we have prayed to the goddess for relief, and we petition her servants just as frequently. This is the center of Nareva's worship in the North, the heart and soul of the goddess of magic's dominion over our people, and yet..." "Has Tychris always been the center of her worship here?" you ask, trying to steer the conversation away before Tethys loses her grip entirely. [pc.bg acolyte |And before Tethys commits a little too much heresy for her guards to ignore. ] Indeed, she takes another deep, centering breath, and nods. "Since the Godswar. Once, it is said, she kept her home in the Sailgraves — Estelore, perhaps; that old Belharan coven of madmen sprung up around her. But when the world was on the brink of ending, the goddess chosen our city to make her stand at. We were her last bastion in this part of the world, and her compact with us was for her protection everlasting." "But her priests haven't been able to help..." Tethys sighs deeply. "They have tried, goddess bless them. Two of the Order Astrida, including the Order's leader herself, came to our aid... but they failed. Each time, Kythie's curse has returned in mere days. Now they refuse to meet me at all! Every message is returned with platitudes that they are investigating, telling me that in time they will succeed." She clenches her fists. "These childless monks will never understand how I cannot." It might be worth investigating the temple to see why they've been so unhelpful.
rows.translation: 提克里斯有幸成为纳雷瓦在圣城以北地区最宏伟的神庙的守护者，”泰西斯苦涩地说道，“但他们所提供的，只不过是虚假的希望罢了——如果他们真有什么诚意的话！我……”泰西斯突然停住了话，双手紧握成拳。有那么一瞬间，你甚至担心她会失控…… 她我会去敲门，”她说道，但片刻之后，她只是将手掌轻轻放在门上。 “我很抱歉，”泰西斯终于开口说道，“但我对我们那些神圣的庇护者感到无比失望。自从诅咒开始以来，每一天我们都在向女神祈祷，请求她解除这个诅咒，同时也经常向她的仆从们求助。 这里是北方地区人们崇拜纳雷瓦的中心，也是这位魔法女神统治我们人民的象征与灵魂所在……然而…… “那么，提克里斯一直以来都是这里人们崇拜的对象吗？”你问道，试图在泰西斯彻底失去控制之前转移话题。 [pc.bg acolyte |而在泰西斯犯下那些连她的守卫们都无法忽视的“异端行为”之前…… ] 的确，她又深吸一口气，让自己平静下来，然后点了点头。“从神战开始之后。据说，有一段时间她住在赛尔格雷夫斯地区——也许是埃斯托洛尔吧；那时，那些由疯子们组成的贝尔哈兰教团开始围绕在她身边。但是当世界即将走向毁灭的时候，那位被选中的女神…… 我们的 这座城市是她最后的依靠。在我们这个地区，我们是她最后的屏障，而她与我们签订的契约，正是为了获得永恒的保护。” “但是她的祭司们却无能为力……” 泰西斯深深地叹了口气。 他们已经尽力了，愿女神保佑他们。包括组织领袖在内的两名‘阿斯特拉达组织’成员前来帮助我们……但他们失败了。每次，凯西的诅咒都在短短几天内再次降临。现在，他们根本拒绝见我！ 每条回复都只是些敷衍之词，说他们正在调查中，还告诉我只要再等等，他们就会成功。“她紧握着双拳，继续说道：‘这些没有孩子的僧侣永远也不会理解，为什么我无法做到。 或许值得去调查一下那座寺庙，弄清楚为什么它们一直如此毫无帮助。
rows.raw: ' "Tychris bears the honor of hosting Nareva's grandest temple north of the Holy City itself," Tethys says bitterly, "but they have only offered false hope, when they offered any at all! I-" Tethys cuts herself short, balling her fists. For a moment you worry that she'll be the one striking the door, but a heartbeat passes, and she merely rests her palm against the wood. "I apologize," Tethys finally says. "But I have grown more than frustrated with our divine patrons. Each and every day since the curse began, we have prayed to the goddess for relief, and we petition her servants just as frequently. This is the center of Nareva's worship in the North, the heart and soul of the goddess of magic's dominion over our people, and yet..." "Has Tychris always been the center of her worship here?" you ask, trying to steer the conversation away before Tethys loses her grip entirely. [pc.bg acolyte |And before Tethys commits a little too much heresy for her guards to ignore. ] Indeed, she takes another deep, centering breath, and nods. "Since the Godswar. Once, it is said, she kept her home in the Sailgraves — Estelore, perhaps; that old Belharan coven of madmen sprung up around her. But when the world was on the brink of ending, the goddess chosen our city to make her stand at. We were her last bastion in this part of the world, and her compact with us was for her protection everlasting." "But her priests haven't been able to help..." Tethys sighs deeply. "They have tried, goddess bless them. Two of the Order Astrida, including the Order's leader herself, came to our aid... but they failed. Each time, Kythie's curse has returned in mere days. Now they refuse to meet me at all! Every message is returned with platitudes that they are investigating, telling me that in time they will succeed." She clenches her fists. "These childless monks will never understand how I cannot." It might be worth investigating the temple to see why they've been so unhelpful. '

rows.translation: 纳雷瓦神庙

rows.original: "Our palace isn't a fortress, but it is well guarded," Tethys says firmly. "I have trained our armsmen myself, and they are supplied by the best smiths in the North, supported by the most well-educated magicians a city could ever supply, here in the shadow of Nareva's own temple." [pc.bgMulti soldier slumrat |You've heard plenty of nobles say something similar, and get robbed blind all the same. |A fine boast, but well-armed soldiers aren't a deterrent to those skilled enough to slip their notice in the first place. |Fine, fine. ] "So there's no chance anyone had access to Kythie without you knowing?" "Of course not. Our daughter is our pride and joy, and the hope of a nation. No one in Tychris is better protected than Kythie." "And the palace itself? Who controls who comes and goes?" Tethys begins to bristle under the questioning now. "Captain Tyna, of course. She manages the day to day of guarding our persons, the treasury, and the grounds." "From the entire complex?" you ask. "Even the temple? You share your outer walls with it." "... A number of people are permitted in the walls each day, yes, but each is accounted for by night's end. In all my years we have never found temple supplicants meandering the grounds past nightfall. Come now." She's never found any. [pc.cl thief |Despite her insistences, it seems to you like Tychris Palace would be pretty easy to sneak around in. The grounds are open, the palace itself is sprawling and blessed with so, so many windows... it would be child's play to break into if you could sneak past the guards. Or if you had a proper disguise... ]
rows.translation: 我们的宫殿虽然不是堡垒，但防守措施十分严密，”泰西斯坚定地说道，“这些卫兵都是我亲自训练的，他们配备了北方最优秀的铁匠制造的武器，同时还得到了这座城市里最有学识的魔法师们的支持——这一切，都发生在纳雷瓦神庙的庇护之下。 [pc.bgMulti soldier slumrat |你肯定听过很多贵族说类似的话，但最终还是被抢得一干二净。 |说得挺响亮的，不过…… 装备精良的士兵对于那些手段高明、能够悄无声息地完成行动的人来说，根本构不成任何阻碍。. |好吧，好吧。 ] 所以，绝对不可能有人在你不知情的情况下接触到Kythie吧？” “当然不可能。我们的女儿是我们引以为傲的人，也是整个国家的希望。在Tychris，没有谁比Kythie受到更严密的保护。” “那么宫殿本身呢？ 谁来决定谁可以进出这里？” 听到这个问题，泰西斯开始感到不悦了。“当然是泰娜船长了。她负责管理我们的人身安全、财宝以及整个基地的日常事务。” “整个基地的事务都由她来负责吗？你问得倒好……就连那座寺庙也不例外？它的外墙其实与我们共享的。” “……每天确实有很多人被允许进入那片区域，但到了晚上，所有人都必须被清点确认。这些年来，我们从未发现有任何寺庙的信徒在日落后还在这片地方游荡。 快来吧。” 她从来都没有…… 创建 任何。 [pc.cl thief |尽管她一再坚持，但在你看来…… 在提克里斯宫殿里，偷偷行动其实相当容易。场地已经开放，宫殿本身规模庞大，而且拥有无数窗户……如果你能悄悄绕过守卫，或者有合适的伪装，那么闯入宫殿简直易如反掌。 ]
rows.raw: ' "Our palace isn't a fortress, but it is well guarded," Tethys says firmly. "I have trained our armsmen myself, and they are supplied by the best smiths in the North, supported by the most well-educated magicians a city could ever supply, here in the shadow of Nareva's own temple." [pc.bgMulti soldier slumrat |You've heard plenty of nobles say something similar, and get robbed blind all the same. |A fine boast, but well-armed soldiers aren't a deterrent to those skilled enough to slip their notice in the first place. |Fine, fine. ] "So there's no chance anyone had access to Kythie without you knowing?" "Of course not. Our daughter is our pride and joy, and the hope of a nation. No one in Tychris is better protected than Kythie." "And the palace itself? Who controls who comes and goes?" Tethys begins to bristle under the questioning now. "Captain Tyna, of course. She manages the day to day of guarding our persons, the treasury, and the grounds." "From the entire complex?" you ask. "Even the temple? You share your outer walls with it." "... A number of people are permitted in the walls each day, yes, but each is accounted for by night's end. In all my years we have never found temple supplicants meandering the grounds past nightfall. Come now." She's never found any. [pc.cl thief |Despite her insistences, it seems to you like Tychris Palace would be pretty easy to sneak around in. The grounds are open, the palace itself is sprawling and blessed with so, so many windows... it would be child's play to break into if you could sneak past the guards. Or if you had a proper disguise... ] '

rows.translation: 宫殿保安队

rows.original: "Few with power," Tethys says. "We have battled barbarians on and off for generations since the Godswar. Monsters of all kinds, too. But since the Lion Witch's War, we have enjoyed a generation of peace." She pauses, considering. "Although, we must admit, we are not well loved by all out neighbors." Now you're getting somewhere. "Khor'minos, Felurze, Rosetree, even little Hawkethorne... we have good relations with them all. But the new Count of Maidenbridge has been a difficult pill to swallow, and his early reign was tense. If the River Maiden hadn't been flooded at the time, we might have marched to war against him." Oh, this is good. "Why? What did he do?" "Killed the previous count," she says glumly. "Thore was a bandit king, a self-made warlord commanding a band of mercenaries, outlaws, and exiles from across the northern Heartlands. They acted as sellswords to the count, and then turned on him. Burned half the city and killed its rulers without mercy. By the time we'd heard what had happened, that warlord had proclaimed himself the new count! We were allies with Maidenbridge, and overnight they were taken from us!" "But you never did go to war?" Tethys shakes her head. "Evander forbade it, against my counsel. The damage was done, he reasoned, and the best thing we could do for the people of Maidenbridge was to keep the peace, give them the opportunity to rebuild. Still, I marched our soldiers to the border to make sure that his rebellion did not spread. There were skirmishes. Several of the brigands were put to the sword before the we came to terms. But, against all odds, peace did reign. Count Thore proved to be a-" Tethys pauses, frowning, "a capable ruler, not disposed to cruelty towards his subjects. Still, our relationship has remained rocky, and Thore has not shied from showing his frustration in our communiques. More than once I have thought ill tidings that befall the city might have his duplicitous hand behind them, but this would be a new low." Barbarians and a neighboring warlord, all of whom might hold some kind of a grudge...
rows.translation: 掌握权力的人并不多，”泰西斯说道，“自神战以来，我们已经与野蛮人进行了数代人的斗争，也遭遇过各种各样的怪物。但自从狮女巫之战之后，我们终于享受了一代人的和平。” 她停顿了一下，似乎在思考着什么。“不过，我们必须承认，我们并没有受到太多人的喜爱…… 所有 我们的邻国们……” 现在你终于说到点子上了。 “科尔米诺斯、费卢尔泽、罗塞特里，甚至小小的霍克索恩……我们与他们所有人都有着良好的关系。但是，新的梅登布里奇伯爵实在让人难以接受，他在位初期带来的紧张局势令人担忧。如果当时梅登河没有发生洪水，我们或许早就对他发动战争了。 哦，这可真糟糕。‘为什么？他做了什么？’” “他杀了之前的伯爵，”她沮丧地说道，“托雷原本是个土匪头目，一个白手起家的军阀，他率领着一群雇佣兵、逃犯以及来自北方心脏地带的流亡者。他们原本为那位伯爵效力，后来却背叛了他——他们烧毁了半个城市，残忍地杀死了当地的统治者。等我们得知这些事情时，那个军阀已经宣布自己成为了新的伯爵！我们原本与梅登布里奇是盟友，然而一夜之间，他们就被夺走了！ 但是你从来没有真正参加过战争，对吧？” 泰西斯摇了摇头。“埃文德禁止我这么做，尽管我并不认同他的看法。他认为，伤害已经造成了，对我们梅登布里奇的人们来说，最好的做法就是保持和平，给他们重新建设的机会。 尽管如此，我还是率领我们的军队前往边境，以确保他的叛乱不会蔓延。期间发生了一些小规模冲突，有几名叛乱者在我们达成协议之前就被处决了。然而，尽管困难重重，最终还是恢复了和平。 索尔伯爵被证明是一位……泰西斯皱着眉头停顿了一下，“一位有能力的统治者，他并不倾向于对自己的子民施以残酷对待。然而，我们的关系一直很紧张，索尔也在我们的通信中毫不掩饰自己的不满。 我不止一次怀疑，这座城市所遭遇的那些不幸事件，或许都与他的阴险狡诈有关，但这次的行为实在已经达到了新的低点。 那些野蛮人以及邻近的军阀们，他们或许都怀有某种怨恨……
rows.raw: ' "Few with power," Tethys says. "We have battled barbarians on and off for generations since the Godswar. Monsters of all kinds, too. But since the Lion Witch's War, we have enjoyed a generation of peace." She pauses, considering. "Although, we must admit, we are not well loved by all out neighbors." Now you're getting somewhere. "Khor'minos, Felurze, Rosetree, even little Hawkethorne... we have good relations with them all. But the new Count of Maidenbridge has been a difficult pill to swallow, and his early reign was tense. If the River Maiden hadn't been flooded at the time, we might have marched to war against him." Oh, this is good. "Why? What did he do?" "Killed the previous count," she says glumly. "Thore was a bandit king, a self-made warlord commanding a band of mercenaries, outlaws, and exiles from across the northern Heartlands. They acted as sellswords to the count, and then turned on him. Burned half the city and killed its rulers without mercy. By the time we'd heard what had happened, that warlord had proclaimed himself the new count! We were allies with Maidenbridge, and overnight they were taken from us!" "But you never did go to war?" Tethys shakes her head. "Evander forbade it, against my counsel. The damage was done, he reasoned, and the best thing we could do for the people of Maidenbridge was to keep the peace, give them the opportunity to rebuild. Still, I marched our soldiers to the border to make sure that his rebellion did not spread. There were skirmishes. Several of the brigands were put to the sword before the we came to terms. But, against all odds, peace did reign. Count Thore proved to be a-" Tethys pauses, frowning, "a capable ruler, not disposed to cruelty towards his subjects. Still, our relationship has remained rocky, and Thore has not shied from showing his frustration in our communiques. More than once I have thought ill tidings that befall the city might have his duplicitous hand behind them, but this would be a new low." Barbarians and a neighboring warlord, all of whom might hold some kind of a grudge... '

rows.translation: 提克里斯的敌人

rows.original: "You may not see it here, today, Champion, but Tychris is prospering. We are known throughout the world for our glassworks and the skill of our mages, and both bring us wealth and prestige. Our farmlands have had good years, enough to stock the granaries, and trade has continued apace from our neighbors. I struggle to imagine anyone in the city feels betrayed by our rule; certainly not enough to target their future princeps." [pc.bg slumrat courtesan minstrel |An easy analysis from the ivory tower, but you wonder if that's actually true. |Fair enough. ] "So you've had no troubles recently?" "Every city has its troubles!" Tethys says. "We've had some disagreements with the millers' association of late — it's becoming more expensive to import good wood from Hawkethorne, and the millers wish to pass the costs on to our people. The tavernkeepers and farmers alike have turned the court into a gallery for their brawl with the millers." She sighs with frustration. "We've had a series of small bandit raids from beastkin in the mountains, but nothing that our soldiers have not been equal to. The late summer brought flooding into the farmlands, and we are still dealing with the aftermath of that, both the beastkin raids and upset landholders. Which has lead to millers needing more wood to repair the mills after the floods, and... you get the point, I hope. Our squabbles are those of any city." "So aside from the royal family, who're the movers and shakers here?" "The temple, of course, exerts great influence over Tychris. We are Nareva's stronghold, and her worship is paramount among our people. Past that, a number of rural barons control the surrounding countryside — my own father was among them. Today Ruarc en Rhydan is the loudest voice among the county barons, and now the wealthiest. He himself won the argent wreathe in weight lifting, which has furthered his fame quite a bit. Beneath them are the farmers that support the city. The rural commons have flexed greater power these last years, with their growing prosperity; good rain has brought good harvests for a decade, and for the first time in our history we've begun exporting food. Coin flows in from city-states to our south while gems and furs come the northern tundra." So the church and the farmer-barons are both exercising influence outside the court... and the latter are making Tychris even richer by the year. "And what about the Tycathalon?" you ask. "How did that affect things here?" "Our biggest expense every four years," Tethys says, with a hint of pride. "But it is worth it. Athletes from around the region, even across the world, come to earn glory beneath the Temple of Nareva. And it brings travelers from all over to watch, too: merchants, craftsmen, nobles, sorcerers... and all their wealth. For two weeks the city is flooded with double its regular population, there's a celebration that never ends from dusk until dawn — and grand exhibitions of magical prowess in the temple. Even the magicians feel the need to flex their muscles during the Tycathalon." A great big gathering of wealthy and powerful people, right before Kythie caught her curse. The timing can't be a coincidence. "Was there anyone particularly noteworthy in attendance?" "Lady Anthiese of Altenspire, a famous sorceress of Altenspire, and her daughter Therese. Certainly the most notable to us, considering Therese won the argent wreathe after Kythie, and then suffered the first outburst of the curse in front of the whole city. Even if she had not, Therese is one of the rare salamanders born with horns — an eye-turning young lady if ever there was one. She made quite the splash. Even with Kythie, it seems..." Tethys clears her throat. "We also had the honor to host Baron Asher of Felurze, uncle to Kythie's betrothed; Aric Ignafaerus of Khor'minos, the famous javelin thrower who was too drunk to hit his targets this year; Halua of Khor'hofra, high priestess of Lumia; Ser Tristan and Dame Faunra, crusaders all the way from the Dukedom of Claremont who took the aurum wreathe in charioteering; Tajana the Lynx, an archmage illusionist from Callathia; Robert Kalter, an alchemist from Barania who won argent in wrestling... ah, and of course, Countess Arielle of Taelia, one of the many prospective heirs to the Alliance. Though rather than leading in the competition, she was leading most of the drunken revelry throughout the city..." You get it, you get it. "Anybody who'd have reason to want to upset the balance here in Tychris?" "If I could think of any, [pc.name], they'd have been in chains before you arrived." Tethys rests her hands on her hips. "My list of suspects is perilously thin. No one has reason to hurt Kythie of all people! There's a list a league long of worthies who should be courting her favor, but cursing her? I cannot think of why! We have maintained peace and stability for a generation in our domain. To strike at the crown would be to upset all that we have accomplished, and for what? Chaos?" You can certainly think of at least one group back east that would love to spread chaos out here...
rows.translation: 冠军，今天你可能在这里看不到，但提克里斯正在蓬勃发展。我们的玻璃制造业和法师们的精湛技艺让我们闻名于世，这两者都为我们带来了财富与声望。我们的农田也取得了良好的收成，储备了充足的粮食，而与邻国的贸易也持续保持着稳定的发展势头。我实在无法想象这座城市里会有人对我们的统治感到不满；更不可能有人会因此而企图推翻未来的王子。 [pc.bg slumrat courtesan minstrel |从象牙塔里出发，这种分析固然简单易懂，但人们不禁会怀疑：这究竟是不是事实呢？ |有道理。 ] 所以你最近没有遇到什么麻烦吧？” “每个城市都会遇到一些麻烦！”泰西斯说道，“最近我们和磨坊主协会发生了一些争执——从霍克索恩进口优质木材的成本越来越高，而那些磨坊主们想把这些成本转嫁给我们的人们。 酒馆老板和农民们把这片空地当成了他们与磨坊主们斗殴的场所。“她沮丧地叹了口气，“最近山上的一些兽人小股匪徒不断来袭击我们，但我们的士兵完全有能力应对他们。 夏末时节，洪水侵袭了农田，我们至今仍在应对这场灾害带来的后果——既有兽人的袭击，也有土地所有者因此而产生的不满。这些情况导致磨坊主们需要更多的木材来修复被洪水破坏的磨坊。我希望你明白我的意思。我们的争吵其实和其他城市里的争吵没什么两样。” “那么，除了王室之外，这里还有哪些真正有影响力的人物或组织呢？” “当然是寺庙了。它在提克里斯拥有巨大的影响力。我们是纳雷瓦的据点，而人们对她的崇拜在我们这里也是极其重要的。 除此之外，还有许多乡绅控制着周边的乡村地区——我的父亲就是其中之一。如今…… 鲁阿尔克·恩·瑞丹是这些郡级男爵中声音最响亮的一个。而他如今已成为最富有的人。他自己还在举重比赛中赢得了银质奖章，这一成就使他的名声更加显赫。在他们之下，是那些支撑着这座城市的农民。近年来，随着农民们日益富裕，他们的力量也变得更强大了；十年来，良好的降雨带来了丰富的收成，我们历史上第一次开始出口食品。南方的城邦向我们输送货币，而北方的苔原地区则送来了宝石和毛皮。 所以教会和那些拥有大量土地的贵族们都在宫廷之外发挥着影响力……而后者更是让提克里斯的财富逐年增加。“那么提卡塔隆事件又是怎么回事呢？”你可能会问，“它对这里的情况产生了什么影响？这是我们每四年要承担的最大一笔开支，”泰西斯略带自豪地说，“但这一切都是值得的。来自整个地区，甚至来自世界各地的运动员都会来到纳雷瓦神庙下争夺荣耀。同时，也有许多来自各地的游客会前来观看这场盛会：商人们、工匠们、贵族们、法师们……以及他们所有的财富。整整两周的时间里，这座城市里的人口数量会增加到平时的两倍，庆祝活动会从黄昏持续到黎明，从未间断——同时，寺庙里还会举办各种展示魔法技艺的盛大表演。就连那些魔法师们，在这个泰卡塔隆节期间也觉得有必要展示一下自己的本领。" 就在凯西遭到诅咒之前，正好举行了一场聚集了许多富人和权贵们的盛大聚会。这样的时间安排绝非巧合。“在出席的人当中，有没有特别值得注意的人物呢？” “有，那就是阿尔滕斯派的安西丝女士——阿尔滕斯派著名的女巫，以及她的女儿特蕾莎。她们无疑是这场聚会中最引人注目的人物。 我们考虑到特蕾莎在凯西之后获得了银色花环，而且还在全城人的注视下首次遭遇了诅咒的发作……即便没有这些经历，特蕾莎也算是那些天生就长着角的稀有蝾螈之一了——她确实是一位令人瞩目的年轻女士。 她引起了不小的轰动。就连和Kythie在一起的时候，似乎也是如此……” Tethys清了清嗓子，继续说道：“我们还有幸邀请到了Felurze的Asher男爵，他是Kythie未婚夫的叔叔；还有Khor’minos的Aric Ignafaerus，那位著名的标枪投掷手，不过今年他喝得酩酊大醉，连目标都投不中；还有Khor’hofra的Halua，她是Lumia的大祭司；还有来自Claremont公国的Ser Tristan和Dame Faunra，他们是通过马车比赛赢得银质奖牌的十字军战士；还有来自Callathia的Tajana the Lynx，她是一位擅长幻术的大法师；还有来自Barania的Robert Kalter，他通过摔跤比赛赢得了银质奖牌。啊，当然还有泰莉亚的艾莉尔伯爵夫人，她也是其中的一位。 很多 联盟的潜在继承人们。不过，她在整个城市里参与的那些醉酒狂欢活动，反而让这场竞争陷入了混乱…… 你懂我的意思吧，“在提克里斯，究竟有没有人会想要打破这里的平衡呢？” “如果我能想到这样的人的话…… [pc.name]要是你在之前赶到，他们早就被锁在链子里了。”泰西斯双手叉腰说道，“我的嫌疑人名单短得可怜——毕竟没人会有理由伤害凯西啊！应该有一大堆值得尊敬的人争相讨好她才对，但谁会诅咒她呢？ 我想不通为什么！在我们的领地，我们已经维持了整整一代人的和平与稳定。如果攻击王室，那就意味着会破坏我们一直以来所取得的一切成果，而这一切，又是为了什么？只是为了制造混乱吗？ 你肯定能想到，在东边，肯定有某个势力非常乐意在这里制造混乱。..
rows.raw: ' "You may not see it here, today, Champion, but Tychris is prospering. We are known throughout the world for our glassworks and the skill of our mages, and both bring us wealth and prestige. Our farmlands have had good years, enough to stock the granaries, and trade has continued apace from our neighbors. I struggle to imagine anyone in the city feels betrayed by our rule; certainly not enough to target their future princeps." [pc.bg slumrat courtesan minstrel |An easy analysis from the ivory tower, but you wonder if that's actually true. |Fair enough. ] "So you've had no troubles recently?" "Every city has its troubles!" Tethys says. "We've had some disagreements with the millers' association of late — it's becoming more expensive to import good wood from Hawkethorne, and the millers wish to pass the costs on to our people. The tavernkeepers and farmers alike have turned the court into a gallery for their brawl with the millers." She sighs with frustration. "We've had a series of small bandit raids from beastkin in the mountains, but nothing that our soldiers have not been equal to. The late summer brought flooding into the farmlands, and we are still dealing with the aftermath of that, both the beastkin raids and upset landholders. Which has lead to millers needing more wood to repair the mills after the floods, and... you get the point, I hope. Our squabbles are those of any city." "So aside from the royal family, who're the movers and shakers here?" "The temple, of course, exerts great influence over Tychris. We are Nareva's stronghold, and her worship is paramount among our people. Past that, a number of rural barons control the surrounding countryside — my own father was among them. Today Ruarc en Rhydan is the loudest voice among the county barons, and now the wealthiest. He himself won the argent wreathe in weight lifting, which has furthered his fame quite a bit. Beneath them are the farmers that support the city. The rural commons have flexed greater power these last years, with their growing prosperity; good rain has brought good harvests for a decade, and for the first time in our history we've begun exporting food. Coin flows in from city-states to our south while gems and furs come the northern tundra." So the church and the farmer-barons are both exercising influence outside the court... and the latter are making Tychris even richer by the year. "And what about the Tycathalon?" you ask. "How did that affect things here?" "Our biggest expense every four years," Tethys says, with a hint of pride. "But it is worth it. Athletes from around the region, even across the world, come to earn glory beneath the Temple of Nareva. And it brings travelers from all over to watch, too: merchants, craftsmen, nobles, sorcerers... and all their wealth. For two weeks the city is flooded with double its regular population, there's a celebration that never ends from dusk until dawn — and grand exhibitions of magical prowess in the temple. Even the magicians feel the need to flex their muscles during the Tycathalon." A great big gathering of wealthy and powerful people, right before Kythie caught her curse. The timing can't be a coincidence. "Was there anyone particularly noteworthy in attendance?" "Lady Anthiese of Altenspire, a famous sorceress of Altenspire, and her daughter Therese. Certainly the most notable to us, considering Therese won the argent wreathe after Kythie, and then suffered the first outburst of the curse in front of the whole city. Even if she had not, Therese is one of the rare salamanders born with horns — an eye-turning young lady if ever there was one. She made quite the splash. Even with Kythie, it seems..." Tethys clears her throat. "We also had the honor to host Baron Asher of Felurze, uncle to Kythie's betrothed; Aric Ignafaerus of Khor'minos, the famous javelin thrower who was too drunk to hit his targets this year; Halua of Khor'hofra, high priestess of Lumia; Ser Tristan and Dame Faunra, crusaders all the way from the Dukedom of Claremont who took the aurum wreathe in charioteering; Tajana the Lynx, an archmage illusionist from Callathia; Robert Kalter, an alchemist from Barania who won argent in wrestling... ah, and of course, Countess Arielle of Taelia, one of the many prospective heirs to the Alliance. Though rather than leading in the competition, she was leading most of the drunken revelry throughout the city..." You get it, you get it. "Anybody who'd have reason to want to upset the balance here in Tychris?" "If I could think of any, [pc.name], they'd have been in chains before you arrived." Tethys rests her hands on her hips. "My list of suspects is perilously thin. No one has reason to hurt Kythie of all people! There's a list a league long of worthies who should be courting her favor, but cursing her? I cannot think of why! We have maintained peace and stability for a generation in our domain. To strike at the crown would be to upset all that we have accomplished, and for what? Chaos?" You can certainly think of at least one group back east that would love to spread chaos out here... '

rows.translation: 城市现状

rows.original: You can see the way this is pushing Tethys to her limits, just the same as Kythie herself, and something about seeing a mother in distress — even one that looks big enough to smother you with her body — sends a pang through your heart. You take a step closer and rest a hand on Tethys's shoulder. "It's going to be alright," you tell her. I will find a way to help your daughter. I promise. So just hold it together a little while longer." "I-" the noblewoman's voice catches in her throat, and she winces back from you — though not enough to brush your hand off completely. [pc.isCunning |Not used to such forwardness, is she? ]"Th-thank you, Champion. I am trying." She says that with such heaviness, you can feel her shrink a little in front of you. "You [pc.hasKids |have children of your own?" You nod. "I pray you never suffer anything like this. Watching your child suffer is an acute horror, and being impotent to help her is... it feels as though I am suffocating. Drowning. I fear you are my only way out. So, one parent to another: please, in the goddess's name, do whatever you must to help her." |don't have children of your own, do you?" You shake your head. "No." "Then you will not understand, not truly. How can you? A child rewrites your life, your very soul. And now I must watch mine suffer, impotent to help her. I feel so completely helpless here, as though I'm drowning in despair." ] "You called me," you assure her. "You took a risk for her, and I'll make sure it pays off. I swear it." Thethys finally relaxes, at least a little bit. "Thank you, Champion. You are right. Even still, one wonders how the world might be different, if I had pursued the path of a priest or sorceress rather than the blade and shield. Could I have found her cure myself, without resorting to such... such debasing means?" "Is this really so bad?" you ask. "Sex is as natural a cure as it gets." The [silly |milfmander |salamander matron ] sighs. "Not like this. Sex is meant to be between lovers, not a... a fleeting dalliance, and certainly not between strangers. If you had courted my daughter, perhaps things would be different, but there is something deeply humiliating about surrendering her to a foreigner who'd never set foot in our lands. Yet that is what makes you perfect to be her savior. Ironic, isn't it?" She's got a very romanticized view of sex, for such a beautiful woman. "I don't want to humiliate you or Kythie, Tethys." "I know," she says. "And yet... I feel as though I've done you as much a dishonor as my daughter. Even if you have a more... libertine view of things than I." The look she gives you seems genuinely regretful, despite how [pc.isDK |ridiculous |outlandish ] the situation is. You squeeze her shoulder a bit tighter. "It'll be alright." "I... thank you." Tethys crosses her arms and glances towards the door.
rows.translation: 你可以看到，这种处境正在将泰西斯逼向极限，就如同凯西本人一样。而看到一位母亲处于困境之中——哪怕她的身躯如此庞大，仿佛足以将你压垮——总会让人心中感到一阵揪痛。你走上一步，将手放在泰西斯的肩上，说道：“一切都会好起来的。 会 想办法帮助你的女儿吧。我保证的。所以，请再坚持一段时间吧。” “我——”那位贵族妇女的声音在喉咙里哽住了，她微微退开了些距离——不过还是没有完全拉开与你之间的距离。 [pc.isCunning |她应该还不习惯这种直率吧？ ]谢谢你，冠军。我正在努力。” 她说这话时的语气如此沉重，以至于你能感觉到她在你面前仿佛缩小了一点点。“你…… [pc.hasKids |你也有自己的孩子吧？” 你点了点头。 “我祈祷你永远不会经历这样的事情。看着自己的孩子受苦，是一种极其痛苦的感觉，而却无能为力去帮助她……那种感觉就像是我自己正在窒息，正在溺水。我担心，你可能是我唯一的希望。所以，作为一位父母，我恳求你：看在女神的份上，无论如何都要帮助她。 |你自己没有孩子，对吧？” 你摇了摇头。“没有。” “那么你就不会真正理解。你怎么可能理解呢？一个孩子的诞生会彻底改变你的人生，甚至改变你的灵魂。而现在，我只能眼睁睁看着自己的孩子受苦，却无能为力。我感到自己完全无助，就像正在绝望中沉沦一样。 ] 是你召唤了我的，”你向她保证，“你为她冒了风险，而我一定会让这一切得到回报。我发誓。” 塞西斯终于放松了下来，至少稍微放松了一些。“谢谢你，冠军。你说得对。不过，人们还是会忍不住想：如果我当初选择了成为牧师或女巫，而不是拿起剑和盾，这个世界会变成什么样呢？ 难道我就不能自己找到治愈她的方法，而不必采用这种……这种卑鄙的手段吗？” “真的有那么糟糕吗？”你问道，“性爱本身就是最自然的治愈方式啊。 [silly |米尔夫曼德 |火蜥蜴女管家 ] 唉，不是这样的。性爱应该是恋人之间的事情，而不是……短暂的逢场作戏，更不应该发生在陌生人之间。如果你当初追求过我的女儿，或许事情会不同吧，但把她交给一个从未踏足过我们这片土地的外国人，确实是一件非常羞辱人的事。然而，正因为如此，你才最适合成为她的救星。真是具有讽刺意味，不是吗？” 对于这样一个美丽的女性来说，她的性观念未免太过浪漫化了。“我并不想让你感到羞辱…… 或者 基西，特提斯。” “我知道，”她说，“然而……我觉得我对你造成的羞辱，和我对女儿造成的羞辱一样深。即使你对这些事情的看法，比我更为……开放。” 她看你的眼神中充满了真正的懊悔，尽管…… [pc.isDK |荒谬至极 |荒诞不经的 ] 情况是这样的。你把她的肩膀再按紧一点，然后说：“会没事的。” “我……谢谢你。” 泰西斯双臂交叉，目光朝门口的方向望去。

rows.translation: 下一个

rows.original:
[flags.P_DILEMMA_CAIT_VISIT
        |"Can we talk to Kythie?" you ask, looking between Cait and Tethys.
        
        "They can!" the princess says from inside her cell. "I'm good for it." 
        
        Tethys gives you and your priestess a weary smile before unbarring the door. "You have your answer then, Champion."
        
        "We're coming in," Cait says gently. "Both of us." 
        
        Kythie's sitting on the bed's edge when you step in, and tries not to wince when the door's bar slides back into place. "Hey, Cait. [pc.name]. How's your investigation going?"
        
        "It's going," you assure her. "How are you feeling?"
        
        She sighs. "I'm okay as I can be. Cait's been a big help." Kythie's tail curls around the cleric's leg, pulling her into princess's lap. "But I feel like I'm going a little crazy in here! I haven't been outside in... in who knows how long. I miss the sun!"
        
        Cait rubs the 'mander's shoulders. "We'll find a fix, sweet thing. Trust [pc.name]. I know I do."
        
        Kythie gives you a hopeful look.
        |You exchange a look with Cait. "I think we're ready to talk to the princess." 
        
        Tethys nods. "She's been more calm, thanks to Sister Caitriss's efforts. I think she's in a state of mind to entertain visitors."
        
        "I am!" a voice says through the door. "I'm okay. Well, okay enough to talk. You can send them in."
        
        You give Tethys a nod, and the lady-consort unbars the door. 
        
        You and Cait step inside. This time, the princess isn't a sweaty mess of naked amazon — rather, she's dressed similarly to her father, with a short kilt and a tunic that leaves most of her chest bare. The effect on a large-breasted woman is... quite a bit more striking than on the willowy Prince Evander. A pair of thick cloth straps are trying to compete with the weight of her chest to hold it up, but is clearly struggling. The princess is quickly settling a fur mantle around her shoulders when you step inside, and her auroran tail has drooped behind her like a shamed puppy. 
        
        "Um, hi," she says awkwardly. Her cheeks and tail both burn a little brighter as you take her new appearance in, but she makes herself look you both in the eye. "Cait, right? And... you're [pc.name]? The Champion she talked about?"
        
        You nod. "That's right."
        
        "I'm Kythie. And I'm faring quite a lot better thanks to... whatever it was you call that."
        
        "Mallach's seventh lotus," Cait smirks. "Also just, a lot of sex. Sweaty, raunchy sex."
        
        The princess's cheeks darken. "I guess it was, at that! Um, thank you. I mean, not for- I mean for helping! I feel more like myself."
        
        Cait squeezes her shoulder. "I'm glad you're doing better."
        
        "Better, not... okay." Kythie's toe-claws tap anxiously on the tower floor. "My insides are still burning, just not so hot as before. Thank the goddess!" She laughs awkwardly. "I don't... it's getting worse. I can feel it from my toes to my t— my tail-tip! Yeah!"
        
        Then you should probably conduct an investigation while you have the chance...
    ]
rows.translation:
[flags.P_DILEMMA_CAIT_VISIT
        |我们能和Kythie谈谈吗？”你问道，目光在Cait和Tethys之间游移。

“她们可以！”公主从她的牢房里说道，“我没意见。”

Tethys疲惫地对你和你的女祭司笑了笑，然后打开了门。“那么，冠军，你已经得到了答案。”

“我们进去吧，”Cait轻声说道，“我们两个都进去。”

当你走进去时，Kythie正坐在床边。当门重新关上时，她努力忍住了抽痛的表情。“嘿，Cait。 [pc.name]你的调查进展如何了？”

“进展还算顺利，”你安慰她道，“你最近感觉怎么样？”

她叹了口气：“还算可以吧。Cait帮了我大忙。”Kythie的尾巴缠在牧师的腿上，把她拉到了公主的怀里，“不过我觉得自己快要疯掉了……我已经很久没有出门了，不知道有多久。我太想念阳光了！”

Cait轻轻拍了拍她的肩膀：“我们会想办法解决的，亲爱的。相信我。 [pc.name]我知道我是这么想的。”

凯西用充满希望的眼神看着你。
        |你与凯特对视了一眼。“我想我们现在可以去和公主谈话了。”

泰西斯点了点头。“多亏了凯特丽丝修女的努力，公主现在平静了许多。我认为她现在已经处于可以接待访客的状态了。是我！”一个声音从门外传来，“我没事，至少现在可以说话了。你们可以把他们让进来吧。”

你向泰西斯点了点头，那位女侍从便打开了门。

你和凯特走了进去。 这一次，公主并不是那个浑身是汗、一丝不挂的亚马逊女战士——相反，她的穿着与她的父亲非常相似，穿着一条短裙和一件上半身几乎完全暴露的束腰外衣。对于一个胸部丰满的女性来说，这样的装扮效果……确实要比对于身材修长的埃万德王子来说更加引人注目。 一对厚实的布带试图承受她胸部的重量，将其固定住，但显然它们正在竭力抵抗。当你走进房间时，公主迅速将一件毛皮披风披在肩上，而她那条极光色的尾巴则像一只受羞的小狗一样耷拉在身后。 
        
        嗯，嗨，”她尴尬地说道。当你注意到她的新模样时，她的脸颊和尾巴都显得更加鲜艳了，但她还是努力让自己直视着你们的眼睛。“你是凯特，对吧？还有……你是…… [pc.name]就是她提到的那位冠军吗？”

你点了点头：“没错。”

“我是凯西。多亏了……你刚才说的那个东西，我的情况好多了。”

“马拉赫的第七朵莲花，”凯特笑着说。 还有，就是很多性爱场景。那种充满汗水、充满激情的性爱。”

公主的脸颊顿时涨红了。“原来是这样啊！嗯，谢谢你。我不是说因为……我是说，因为你的帮助，我终于感觉自己像自己了。”

凯特轻轻拍了拍她的肩膀。 我很高兴你的情况有所好转。”

“好多了，不过……还算不错吧。”凯西的脚趾不安地敲打着塔楼的地板。“我的内脏还是觉得火烧一样疼，只是没有以前那么厉害了。多亏了女神！”她尴尬地笑了笑。 我不……情况正在恶化。我从脚趾到尾巴尖都能感觉到！没错！”

那么，你最好趁现在还有机会，赶紧展开调查吧……
    ]

rows.original: You lean in against the [kythie.lowLibido |sweet |lusty salamander ] princess and run a hand up her thigh, fingers teasing around the divide between scales and sun-warmed skin. Kythie[kythie.lowLibido |'s frilled ears twitch. "Mmm. Something on your mind, [pc.name]?" [pc.isCunning |"Just that I don't want to see you lose control again," you say, easing your hand upwards. "And that some preventative medicine might be in order." "Oh, a doctor are we?" Kythie laughs. She spreads her legs invitingly and leans into you in return, starting to thread her fingers through your [pc.armor]. "You don't much look the part." A few tugs, and she's got your [pc.chest] bare. |"Just how beautiful you are," you say, easing your hand upwards. "And that it would be a shame if we had to wait until-" "Don't be a flirt," Kythie laughs, interrupting you with a darting kiss on the cheek. "I might grow to like it." rows.original:
"Do you think you could answer a few questions for me?"
    
    "Of course, I'll do anything! I mean, anything to help." Kythie swishes her tail and flops back on the bed. "Nareva says that knowledge is the sword that pierces the dark of ignorance! And, goddess, do I feel like I'm drowning in the dark. Ask away."
rows.translation:
你觉得你能帮我回答几个问题吗？”

“当然可以，我什么都会做！只要是能帮忙的事，我一定尽力。”凯西摇着尾巴，重新躺回床上。“纳雷瓦说过，知识就是那把能刺破无知黑暗的利剑！而且，女神啊，我现在真的感觉自己被黑暗所包围……尽管问吧。

rows.original: "It was subtle, at first. I think I first noticed something was changing right after the Tychathalon — that's the big athletics tournament we host every fall. People from all over come to compete! This was only my second year competing, but I won the aurum wreath for discus throwing!" The princess swells with pride, tail-flame blazing like the northern lights. "I was over the moon when mom placed it on my brow, I just... I was overwhelmed. Like my heart was on fire." Kythie's ears twitch. "I don't know what came over me — well, I do now, I guess — but without thinking I grabbed the girl who'd won the argent and kissed her, right on the podium." Her cheeks flush. "Everybody applauded, even if she was not best pleased. How would they know, right? "Dad chewed me out about it, but that was it. Everyone thought I was just excited. Heck, I did too." [var0 |"The other girl, what happened to her?" Cait asks. "Not much on the podium, but when we all got out of sight of the crowd she headbutt me." Kythie manages to laugh. "She was from, um, what was it — Altenspire? Yeah, she was a big deal down there, because she was born with horns. Rare for a 'mander. She'd come a long way to compete and was pretty mad she lost to me by just an inch and a quarter. Turns out she wasn't afraid to use those skullshakers of hers! I had a bruise on my noggin for a week." ] "So what happened next?" you ask. "Nothing, for a while. That big blizzard rolled in a few days later, and everyone who hadn't left town was pretty much trapped indoors. And-" "Big blizzard?" "Yeah! It was crazy. I've never seen a storm that bad roll in so early in the year. It took everyone by surprise. [var0 |You exchange a look with Cait. That's got to be the same as the day you two met, when Calla was kidnapped. |That sounds like the one from the night you met Cait. When Calla was kidnapped. ] "How long exactly after the Tychathalon was this?" Kythie furrows her brow. "Five days, I think?" [pc.isCunning |More than enough time for someone to get back to Hawkethorne from here, if the cult's involved. ] "Did anything change during the storm?" "Yeah," Kythie says. She [kythie.lowLibido |makes an embarrassed sound in her throat and droops her ears |bites her lip with a horny little smirk ]. "I'll say. I, uh, spent the entire time locked in my room knuckles-deep in myself. Thinking about that girl I'd kissed, my fiancee, the chubby harpy lady from the temple, the big muscly priest who'd officiated the Tycathalon with his whole chest out... it was [silly |real degen hours |just a week of degeneracy ] while everyone else was distracted with the tournament's aftermath. It wasn't until a couple days after the storm passed that anybody really noticed I was behaving strangely. Even me! I thought... well, I'd heard that a lot of Tycathalon athletes act promiscuously afterwards. Maybe it was hormonal, from the stress and the rush of victory." "What changed your mind?" Again, you're met with a [kythie.lowLibido |shamefaced expression |shameless look of lust ]. "One of the palace serving-girls walked in on me. In the moment, it seemed entirely natural to just spread my legs wider and crook my tail, like, 'come on in, let's have some fun!' The idea that what I was doing was wholly inappropriate never even entered my mind. I guess I was luck that she'd been with the palace a good long while, and recognize I wasn't myself. She ran off and told my parents immediately. I didn't even have the good sense to be embarrassed about it... or close the door." Kythie rests her elbows on her knees and sighs. "After that it becomes kind of a blur. I remember getting yelled at by both my parents. Then I was waking up in the night to, uh, tend my needs. Waking up in a cold sweat, body on fire. I tried to grab an armsman who was walking past my room. I got in a fistfight with a couple of men when that didn't work. Then Captain Tyna was wrestling me down, and the next thing I know I'm in the tower, screaming and thrashing because everything felt like it was burning, starting in my loins." She gives [var0 |Cait a thankful smile. "I'd still be that way if it wasn't for you, Cait." "My pleasure, princess! You're a handful, but worth it." |you a thankful smile. "I'd still be that way if it wasn't for you, [pc.name]." You squeeze her shoulder. "We'll get you through this." ]
rows.translation: 起初，这种变化很微妙。我记得是在提卡塔隆比赛之后，我才第一次注意到有些事情正在发生变化——那是我们每年秋天举办的盛大体育赛事，来自全国各地的人们都会前来参赛！那是我第二次参加这项比赛，但我竟然在铁饼项目中赢得了金质奖环！公主感到无比自豪，她的尾焰如同北极光般闪耀。”当妈妈把那枚徽章戴在我的额头上时，我简直欣喜若狂，我……简直被这种感觉冲昏了头脑，感觉我的心都快要燃烧起来了。” 凯西的耳朵微微一动：“当时我也不知道自己是怎么做的——嗯，现在想来，我大概知道原因了——但我没多想，就抓住了那个赢得银质徽章的女孩，在领奖台上亲了她一下。她的脸颊涨红了。尽管她这样，所有人还是鼓起了掌来。 不是 开心极了。他们怎么可能知道呢，对吧？“爸爸因此训了我一顿，但也就仅此而已。大家都以为我只是太兴奋了。说实话，我当时确实也是这么觉得的。 [var0 |另一个女孩，她后来怎么样了？”凯特问道。 “在台上时没什么事情发生，但当我们所有人都离开人群的视线后，她用头撞了我。”凯西努力笑了笑，“她来自……嗯，应该是阿尔滕斯派尔吧？对，她在那里算是挺有地位的，因为她天生就长着角。对于一个‘曼德尔’来说，这种情况相当罕见。她专程赶来参加比赛，结果仅仅差了一英寸半就输给了我，她当时肯定气坏了。后来我发现，她根本不怕使用那些头槌啊！我的脑袋上还淤青了一周呢。 ] 那么接下来发生了什么？”你问道。 “一开始什么也没发生。几天后，一场大暴风雪来了，所有没有离开城镇的人几乎都被困在了家里。然后——” “大暴风雪？” “没错！那场暴风雪简直太可怕了。我从来没见过在一年这么早的时候会出现这么猛烈的风暴，它让所有人都措手不及。 [var0 |你与凯特对视了一眼。那一刻，一定让人回想起你们两人初次相遇的那一天，也就是卡拉被绑架的那一刻。 |那应该就是你在那个晚上遇到Cait、也就是Calla被绑架的那个晚上发生的事情吧。 ] 这到底是在提卡萨隆事件发生后的多久？” 凯西皱起了眉头：“我想是五天吧？ [pc.isCunning |如果邪教组织牵涉其中的话，从这里回到霍克索恩完全有足够的时间。 ] 在暴风雨期间，有什么变化吗？” “是的，”凯西说。她 [kythie.lowLibido |她喉咙里发出尴尬的声音，然后把耳朵耷拉了下来。 |她咬着嘴唇，脸上带着一丝淫荡的笑容。 ]我得说，那段时间我几乎一直把自己关在房间里，沉浸在自己的思绪中。我一直在想着那个我吻过的姑娘，我的未婚妻，还有寺庙里那个胖乎乎的‘女妖’，还有那位在仪式上挺起胸膛主持仪式的肌肉发达的祭司…… [silly |真正的堕落时光 |仅仅一周的时间，就陷入了堕落的境地…… ] 就在其他所有人都被锦标赛的余波所吸引、无暇他顾的时候，直到风暴过去几天后，才有人真正注意到我的行为有些异常。就连我自己也沒有察觉！我当时想……嗯，我听说过很多参加泰卡塔隆锦标赛的运动员在比赛结束后都会表现出一些异常行为。也许这是由于荷尔蒙的影响，再加上比赛带来的压力以及胜利带来的兴奋吧。 “是什么让你改变了想法呢？ [kythie.lowLibido |面露羞愧之色 |毫无羞耻之心的贪婪目光 ]有个宫女撞见了我，当时我简直觉得…… 完全地 我当时只是自然而然地张开双腿，摇摇尾巴，仿佛在说：“进来吧，我们一起玩吧！”根本没想过自己的行为有什么不妥。或许我运气比较好，因为她已经在宫殿里待了很长时间，所以能认出我当时并不是平时的自己。 她跑出去，立刻把这件事告诉了我的父母。我甚至都没有足够的理智去为此感到尴尬……或者把门关上。 凯西把肘部放在膝盖上，叹了口气：“从那以后，发生的事情就有点模糊不清了。 我记得当时父母都对我大发雷霆。后来我经常在半夜醒来，去解决一些生理需求。每次醒来时都会满身大汗，感觉身体仿佛着了火一样。有一次，我试图抓住路过我房间的某个男人，但没能成功，结果就和几个男人打了起来。 然后泰娜队长开始把我按倒在地，接下来我就发现自己在了塔里，不停地尖叫、挣扎，因为我的整个身体仿佛都在燃烧，疼痛从腰部开始蔓延开来。 [var0 |凯特露出了感激的微笑：“如果没有你，我可能至今还是那样。” “能帮上你的忙，是我的荣幸，公主！你虽然有点难伺候，但确实值得这个付出。 |你露出了感激的微笑：“如果没有你，我依然会是这样。 [pc.name]你轻轻拍了拍她的肩膀：“我们会帮你度过这个难关的。 ]

I had a bruise on my noggin for a week." ] "So what happened next?" you ask. "Nothing, for a while. That big blizzard rolled in a few days later, and everyone who hadn't left town was pretty much trapped indoors. And-" "Big blizzard?" "Yeah! It was crazy. I've never seen a storm that bad roll in so early in the year. It took everyone by surprise. [var0 |You exchange a look with Cait. That's got to be the same as the day you two met, when Calla was kidnapped. |That sounds like the one from the night you met Cait. When Calla was kidnapped. ] "How long exactly after the Tychathalon was this?" Kythie furrows her brow. "Five days, I think?" [pc.isCunning |More than enough time for someone to get back to Hawkethorne from here, if the cult's involved. ] "Did anything change during the storm?" "Yeah," Kythie says. She [kythie.lowLibido |makes an embarrassed sound in her throat and droops her ears |bites her lip with a horny little smirk ]. "I'll say. I, uh, spent the entire time locked in my room knuckles-deep in myself. Thinking about that girl I'd kissed, my fiancee, the chubby harpy lady from the temple, the big muscly priest who'd officiated the Tycathalon with his whole chest out... it was [silly |real degen hours |just a week of degeneracy ] while everyone else was distracted with the tournament's aftermath. It wasn't until a couple days after the storm passed that anybody really noticed I was behaving strangely. Even me! I thought... well, I'd heard that a lot of Tycathalon athletes act promiscuously afterwards. Maybe it was hormonal, from the stress and the rush of victory." "What changed your mind?" Again, you're met with a [kythie.lowLibido |shamefaced expression |shameless look of lust ]. "One of the palace serving-girls walked in on me. In the moment, it seemed entirely natural to just spread my legs wider and crook my tail, like, 'come on in, let's have some fun!' The idea that what I was doing was wholly inappropriate never even entered my mind. I guess I was luck that she'd been with the palace a good long while, and recognize I wasn't myself. She ran off and told my parents immediately. I didn't even have the good sense to be embarrassed about it... or close the door." Kythie rests her elbows on her knees and sighs. "After that it becomes kind of a blur. I remember getting yelled at by both my parents. Then I was waking up in the night to, uh, tend my needs. Waking up in a cold sweat, body on fire. I tried to grab an armsman who was walking past my room. I got in a fistfight with a couple of men when that didn't work. Then Captain Tyna was wrestling me down, and the next thing I know I'm in the tower, screaming and thrashing because everything felt like it was burning, starting in my loins." She gives [var0 |Cait a thankful smile. "I'd still be that way if it wasn't for you, Cait." "My pleasure, princess! You're a handful, but worth it." |you a thankful smile. "I'd still be that way if it wasn't for you, [pc.name]." You squeeze her shoulder. "We'll get you through this." ] '

rows.translation: 《诅咒的起源》

rows.original: "I don't think so! Not that could do something like this, anyway!" Kythie sighs. "I'm sure Mom's been tearing her scales off thinking about every bandit lord and barbarian chieftain she's crossed blades with as Strategos. Our family used to fight giants, generations ago, but they've barely been seen in the last century — and I don't think they'd stoop to cursing." "Would a bandit or barbarian?" [pc.bg barbarian |You know your tribe had shamans, and you vaguely remember stories of clashing with Tychrisian soldiers decades ago, but that doesn't seem like something any of your people would do. ] Kythie shakes her head. "Maybe a rogue sorcerer? I hear those are always a problem down south, but the Order Astrida always hunt them down in the end. I know Mom killed one right when I was born. Maybe he had an apprentice or something, but... I don't know. Really we're pretty safe up here. We're friends with most of our neighbors. I don't know who'd be so mad at our family they'd try to hurt me." "If not your family, what about you personally? Do you know of anybody holding a grudge?" "Me?" Kythie seems genuinely taken aback by the question. "What did I do?" [var0 |Cait gives her a disarming smile. "Nothing, sweetheart. But sometimes people take innocent things so far the wrong way it drives them mad. So try and think about anybody whose behavior around you changed, or something like that." |You sigh. "That's what I'm trying to find out. Did somebody start treating you differently lately?" ] The princess pouts. "No! I don't know. The girl from Altenspire I beat out in the Tycathalon, Ariande, was righteously pissed off at me, but she's as much of a meathead as I am!" Kythie frowns. "Now that you mention it, her mother's a court magician in their home city, I think. I guess she could have, but why...?" "We don't know anything for certain," you say. [pc.isCunning |But Kythie's prime competition being a magician's daughter is a possible lead. The curse could have been intended to weaken Kythie before the competition just as easily as it could have been meant to punish her for winning... ] "Anybody else you can think of?" "I guess, uh, there's a girl down at the temple library. Ynes? The last couple times I've been there, I think she was flirting with me! She used to be a witch herself, I hear. Before she came to the temple." "Did you reciprocate?" Kythie shakes her head vigorously. "No, never. I would never. I won't pretend she's not beautiful, but there's something unsettling about her. Not to mention she's a thrall to the temple. I couldn't..." [pc.isCunning |Still, if a witch feels spurned... ] "And nobody else comes to mind?" "No," Kythie sighs. "Tychris is full of spellcasters, [pc.name]. Magister Vasko lives here in the palace, he'd know more of them than I do! Ask him..."
rows.translation: 我可不这么认为！反正那些人也不会做出这种事来！”凯西叹道，“我敢肯定，妈妈现在肯定在回想那些她以战略家的身份与之交战的强盗头目和野蛮人首领们，心里肯定在懊恼不已。我们家族在几代前还与巨人战斗过，但在过去的一个世纪里，几乎再也没见过巨人的踪迹了——而且我觉得他们也不会沦落到诅咒别人的地步。” “那强盗或者野蛮人会这么做吗？ [pc.bg barbarian |你知道你们的部落中有萨满，也隐约记得几十年前与提克里西亚士兵发生过冲突的故事，但这种行为似乎不符合你们族人的性格。 ] 基西摇了摇头：“也许是个叛逃的巫师吧？我听说在南方，这类人总是会惹麻烦，但阿斯特里达教团最终总会把他们抓起来。我记得我出生的时候，妈妈就杀死了其中一个。也许他还有学徒之类的，不过……我真的不知道。其实我们在这里相当安全，我们和大多数邻居都是朋友。我想不出究竟会是谁对我们家怀有如此深的恨意，以至于想要伤害我们。 我如果不是你的家人，那你自己呢？你有没有认识的人在心里怨恨你什么？” “我？”凯西似乎真的被这个问题弄糊涂了。“我做了什么吗？ [var0 |凯特向她露出了一个让人放松的微笑：“没什么，亲爱的。但有时候，人们会把一些无恶意的举动误解得太过分，从而导致他们发怒。所以，试着想想那些在你身边，行为发生了变化的人吧。 |你叹了口气：“这正是我想弄清楚的。最近是不是有人开始对你另眼相待了？ ] 公主撅起了嘴：“不！我真的不知道。在泰卡塔隆比赛中我打败的那个来自阿尔滕斯派尔的姑娘，阿里安德，她当时气得够呛，但她和我也一样蠢啊！”凯西皱了皱眉头：“你说得对，我记得她妈妈在他们家乡城是个宫廷法师。也许她本来可以帮上忙的，但为什么……？” “我们确实什么都不确定，”你说道。 [pc.isCunning |但凯西的主要竞争对手是一名魔术师的女儿，这个信息或许能提供一些线索。 这个诅咒本可以轻易地在比赛之前削弱凯西的力量，也同样可以用来惩罚她赢得比赛…… ] 还有其他人你能想到的吗？” “我想，呃，寺庙图书馆里有个女孩。叫Ynes吧？我上次去那里的时候，感觉她在跟我调情呢！听说她以前也是一名女巫，是在来到寺庙之前。” “你有没有回应她？” Kythie拼命地摇着头：“没有，绝对没有。我永远不会那么做的。我得承认她很漂亮，但她身上有种让人不安的感觉。更何况，她还是寺庙的奴仆。我不可能…… [pc.isCunning |然而，如果一位女巫感到被冷落了…… ] 难道没有其他人能想到吗？” “没有，”凯西叹道，“提克里斯城里到处都是施法者…… [pc.name]瓦斯科大师就住在这座宫殿里，他肯定比我知道得更多！去问他吧……

[pc.bg barbarian |You know your tribe had shamans, and you vaguely remember stories of clashing with Tychrisian soldiers decades ago, but that doesn't seem like something any of your people would do. ] Kythie shakes her head. "I guess, uh, there's a girl down at the temple library. Ynes? The last couple times I've been there, I think she was flirting with me! She used to be a witch herself, I hear. Before she came to the temple." "Did you reciprocate?" Kythie shakes her head vigorously. "No, never. I would never. I won't pretend she's not beautiful, but there's something unsettling about her. Not to mention she's a thrall to the temple. I couldn't..." [pc.isCunning |Still, if a witch feels spurned... ] "And nobody else comes to mind?" "No," Kythie sighs. "Tychris is full of spellcasters, [pc.name]. Magister Vasko lives here in the palace, he'd know more of them than I do! Ask him..." '

rows.translation: 她有敌人吗？

rows.original: "No!" Kythie laughs. "I'm as magic as a rock on the side of the road. My parents hired a tutor to teach me spellcraft when I was young, but nothing stuck. Vasky — I mean, Magister Vasko — tried to get me to apprentice to him for a year anyway, but I didn't make it more than a couple of weeks before I had to tell him I couldn't hack it. And I don't give up easy!" "What made you give up?" Kythie shrugs. "I just... couldn't do it. As hard as I tried, it's like my fingers and tongue just won't work the way I tell them when I try and recite an incantation. And it's weird, I'm an athlete, I know how to push my body... except it all falls apart the moment I open a book." [var0 |Cait chuckles. "Some people have a real knack for magic. I'm told I'm one of them! And if some people can have a natural talent, I guess some people have a natural... un-talent." Kythie snorts. "Yeah, it's... something like that, feels like." |[pc.isMage |"Some people just aren't cut out for it," you say. "Spellcasting's hard, and dangerous, and if you try and push yourself to do it anyway you're likely to hurt yourself or others." "Thank you!" Kythie says, throwing a hand up. "I'm terrified of turning myself into a newt or something!" |"Alright," you say, "so you're no mage yourself. And you stopped practicing when?" "Ages back," Kythies says. "More than a year ago." ] ] You scratch your chin. Kythie definitely didn't do this to herself at least.
rows.translation: 不！”凯西笑着说，“我可一点魔法天赋都没有，就像路边的石头一样。我小时候，父母请了老师来教我魔法，但我什么都不会。瓦斯基……我是说，瓦斯科大师，曾经试图让我跟他当一年学徒，但我连几周都坚持不下来，最后只好告诉他我实在不适合学习魔法。 我可不会轻易放弃！” “是什么让你放弃了呢？” 凯西耸了耸肩：“我只是……做不到而已。无论我怎么努力，当我试图念出那些咒语时，我的手指和舌头就是不听使唤，完全无法按照我的意愿来动作。 奇怪的是，我本来是个运动员，知道如何锻炼自己的身体……但只要一拿起书来，所有这些努力就都白费了。 [var0 |凯特轻笑起来：“有些人真的天生就擅长魔法。据说我就是其中之一！而如果有些人天生就具备这种天赋，那么我想也一定有人天生就缺乏这种能力吧。” 凯西哼了一声：“是啊，大概就是这个意思吧。 |[pc.isMage |有些人就是不适合做这种事情，”你说，“施法既困难又危险，如果你强行尝试，很可能会伤害到自己或他人。” “谢谢！”凯西举手说道，“我可害怕把自己变成一只蝾螈之类的东西！ |好吧，”你说，“所以你自己并不是法师。那你是什么时候停止练习的？” “很久以前了，”凯西斯回答，“已经有一年多了。 ] ] 你挠了挠下巴。 基西绝对不是自己这么做的。 至少是这样。
